A.label: Scenario A — Wartime (ERA + Multilateral)
A.description: Trust capitalised from instruments legally operable during active conflict. The ~$280B in frozen Russian sovereign assets remain in G7/EU custodian accounts; their windfall interest (~$3B/yr) backs the ERA extraordinary-revenue loan. Multilateral pledges and EU Facility tranches form the remainder. Russia's principal liability is acknowledged but not yet transferred.
A.legal_basis: G7 Vilnius communiqué (2023); EU Council Regulation 2024/1469 (windfall interest); ERA/G7 framework (2024)

B.label: Scenario B — Post-War (Hague Reparations + Full Transfer)
B.description: Trust capitalised following a post-war legal settlement. The primary source is direct reparations from Russia under an ICJ/Hague tribunal award or negotiated peace treaty — Ukraine's documented damage claim exceeds $486B (RDNA3). A parallel track transfers the frozen $280B principal once G7 legal frameworks permit. Larger corpus, longer mobilisation timeline: 2–5 years from ceasefire.
B.legal_basis: ICJ case Ukraine v. Russia (filed 2022); UN General Assembly Resolution ES-11/5 (reparations); REPO Act (US, 2024); G7 frozen-asset transfer framework
